user_request: Draft a civil suit for recovery of possession of immovable property. Plaintiff is owner of the property situated at Survey No. 45, Koramangala, Bangalore and Defendant is in unauthorized occupation after expiry of permissive possession/lease granted on 01.01.2020 which expired on 31.12.2022. Defendant refusing to vacate despite legal notice dated 15.03.2023. Property value is Rs.50,00,000/-. Monthly rental value is Rs.25,000/-. Seek recovery of possession, mesne profits from 01.01.2023 at Rs.25,000/- per month, and costs of the suit. File before the City Civil Court, Bangalore.

facts.summary: Plaintiff seeks recovery of possession of immovable property (Survey No. 45, Koramangala, Bangalore) from Defendant who is in unauthorized occupation after the expiry of a permissive possession/lease (01.01.2020 to 31.12.2022). Defendant refused to vacate despite a legal notice dated 15.03.2023.

chronology.event: Legal notice issued to Defendant to vacate

evidence.description: Legal notice dated 15.03.2023 requesting Defendant to vacate
Ownership documents for Survey No. 45, Koramangala, Bangalore

draft_artifacts.title: Eviction Plaint
draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT

AT BANGALORE

O.S. No. __________ / 2024

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}},
Aged about {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}},
Aged about {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF POSSESSION OF IMMOVABLE PROPERTY WITH MESNE PROFITS UNDER SECTION 5 OF THE SPECIFIC RELIEF ACT, 1963 READ WITH SECTION 26 OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the suit property, being immovable property bearing Survey No. 45, Koramangala, Bangalore, is situate within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court, and the Defendant is in unauthorized occupation of the said property within such territorial limits.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. 50,00,000/- (Rupees Fifty Lakhs Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court. This Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain suits for recovery of possession of immovable property.

FACTS OF THE CASE

4. The Plaintiff is the absolute owner of the immovable property bearing Survey No. 45, Koramangala, Bangalore, more particularly described in the schedule hereunder written (hereinafter referred to as "the suit property"). The Plaintiff has derived title to the suit property through valid ownership documents (copies of the ownership documents are annexed hereto and marked as Annexure A).

5. The Defendant is an erstwhile tenant whose tenancy stands determined. The Defendant was inducted as a tenant under a lease agreement in respect of the suit property for a period of three years commencing from 01.01.2020 and ending on 31.12.2022 (a copy of the lease/permission document granting permissive possession is annexed hereto and marked as Annexure B).

6. The said lease in favour of the Defendant stood determined and expired by efflux of time on 31.12.2022. Upon expiry of the lease period, the Defendant's right to occupy the suit property ceased, and the Defendant became liable to deliver vacant and peaceful possession of the suit property to the Plaintiff.

7. However, despite the expiry of the lease on 31.12.2022, the Defendant has wrongfully continued in occupation of the suit property without any lawful authority, right or title. The Defendant is in unauthorized occupation of the suit property from 01.01.2023 onwards.

8. The Plaintiff, through his advocate, caused a legal notice dated 15.03.2023 to be served upon the Defendant, calling upon the Defendant to vacate and deliver vacant possession of the suit property to the Plaintiff (a true copy of the legal notice along with the postal receipt and acknowledgment card is annexed hereto and marked as Annexure C).

9. Despite receipt of the said legal notice, the Defendant has failed, neglected and refused to vacate the suit property or to deliver possession thereof to the Plaintiff. The Defendant continues to be in wrongful possession of the suit property without any lawful justification.

10. The monthly rental value of the suit property is Rs. 25,000/-. The Plaintiff is entitled to mesne profits for the wrongful use and occupation of the suit property by the Defendant from 01.01.2023 onwards at the rate of Rs. 25,000/- per month.

11. The Plaintiff has clear title to the suit property and the Defendant's occupation is unauthorized. The Defendant has no right, title or interest in the suit property and is merely a trespasser whose tenancy has been lawfully determined under Section 111 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882.

LEGAL BASIS

12. The Plaintiff submits that he is entitled to recover possession of the suit property under Section 5 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963. Section 5 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963 provides that a person entitled to the possession of specific immovable property may recover the same in the manner prescribed by the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908. In the present case, the Plaintiff, being the owner of the suit property, is entitled to possession thereof, and the Defendant, being an erstwhile tenant whose tenancy has expired, is bound to deliver possession.

13. The Plaintiff further submits that the Defendant was a tenant whose tenancy has been lawfully determined under Section 111 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882. Section 111 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882 provides for the determination of lease, inter alia, by efflux of time. The lease in favour of the Defendant having expired on 31.12.2022, the tenancy stood determined by efflux of time, and the Defendant's continued possession is unauthorized.

14. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882 which provides for the duration of certain leases in the absence of a written contract or local custom. However, in the present case, the lease was for a fixed term which has expired by efflux of time.

15. The Plaintiff further relies upon Section 116 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882 which provides for the effect of holding over after the expiry of the lease. The Defendant, by continuing in possession after the determination of the lease, has become a tenant at will or a tenant holding on a month-to-month basis, and the Plaintiff is entitled to evict the Defendant.

16. The Plaintiff submits that he is entitled to claim mesne profits under Section 2(12) of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 read with Order XX Rule 12 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908. Section 2(12) of the CPC defines mesne profits as profits which the person in wrongful possession of such property actually received or might have received with ordinary diligence from it. The Defendant being in wrongful possession of the suit property, the Plaintiff is entitled to mesne profits at the rate of Rs. 25,000/- per month, being the fair rental value of the suit property.

17. The Plaintiff further relies upon the provisions of the Indian Easements Act, 1882, particularly Sections 60 to 63 thereof, which provide for the extinction and suspension of easements and the right to recover possession. The Plaintiff's right to possession of the suit property is absolute and the Defendant has no easementary right to continue in occupation.

CAUSE OF ACTION

18. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 01.01.2023 when the Defendant, despite the expiry of the lease on 31.12.2022, failed and refused to vacate the suit property and deliver vacant possession thereof to the Plaintiff, thereby continuing in unauthorized occupation and giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to sue for recovery of possession.

19. The cause of action further arose on 15.03.2023 when the Plaintiff, through his advocate, caused a legal notice to be served upon the Defendant calling upon the Defendant to vacate the suit property, and the Defendant despite receipt of the said notice failed and refused to comply with the demand therein.

20. The cause of action is a continuing one as the Defendant continues to be in wrongful possession of the suit property from day to day, and the said cause of action continues to accrue and subsist from day to day within the territorial jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court.

LIMITATION

21. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 65 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of twelve years for a suit for possession of immovable property based on previous ownership or possession when the defendant is in unauthorized occupation after the expiry of a lease. The cause of action having first arisen on 01.01.2023 when the Defendant's tenancy determined, and the suit being filed within the prescribed period of twelve years from the said date, the present suit is well within limitation.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

22. The suit is valued at Rs. 50,00,000/- (Rupees Fifty Lakhs Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee, being the market value of the suit property. The requisite court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- has been paid on this plaint in accordance with the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958, computed on the suit valuation at the applicable ad valorem rate.

MESNE PROFITS

23. The Plaintiff claims mesne profits for the wrongful use and occupation of the suit property by the Defendant as follows:

    (a) Past mesne profits from 01.01.2023 till the date of filing of this suit at the rate of Rs. 25,000/- (Rupees Twenty-Five Thousand Only) per month, being the fair monthly rental value of the suit property;

    (b) Future mesne profits from the date of filing of this suit till the date of delivery of possession at the rate of Rs. 25,000/- (Rupees Twenty-Five Thousand Only) per month or at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems just and reasonable;

    (c) The Plaintiff reserves the right to claim enhanced mesne profits if the actual profits received or receivable by the Defendant are found to be higher than the claimed rate.

PRAYER

24. In the premises stated hereinabove, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be graciously pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree for recovery of possession in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant directing the Defendant to deliver vacant and peaceful possession of the suit property, being immovable property bearing Survey No. 45, Koramangala, Bangalore, more particularly described in the schedule hereunder written;

    (b) Pass a preliminary decree for mesne profits in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for the period from 01.01.2023 till the date of delivery of possession at the rate of Rs. 25,000/- (Rupees Twenty-Five Thousand Only) per month, and direct an inquiry under Order XX Rule 12 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 for determination of the final amount of mesne profits;

    (c) Award costs of this suit including court fees, advocate fees and all pre-litigation costs incurred for issuing the legal notice;

    (d) Grant such other and further relief as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

AND FOR THIS ACT OF KINDNESS, THE PLAINTIFF SHALL, AS IN DUTY BOUND, EVER PRAY.

SCHEDULE OF PROPERTY

All that piece and parcel of immovable property bearing Survey No. 45, situated at Koramangala, Bangalore, more particularly described as follows:

BOUNDARIES:
North : {{BOUNDARY_NORTH}}
South : {{BOUNDARY_SOUTH}}
East : {{BOUNDARY_EAST}}
West : {{BOUNDARY_WEST}}

Measuring: {{PROPERTY_AREA}} sq. ft. approximately.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

| Sr. No. | Description | Annexure |

|---------|-------------|----------|
| 1. | Ownership documents for Survey No. 45, Koramangala, Bangalore | Annexure A |
| 2. | Lease/Permission Document granting permissive possession from 01.01.2020 to 31.12.2022 | Annexure B |
| 3. | Legal notice dated 15.03.2023 with postal receipt and AD card | Annexure C |

VERIFICATION

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby solemnly verify and state on oath that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 24 of this plaint are true and correct to my personal knowledge based upon the records and documents available with me, that the contents relating to legal submissions are based upon legal advice received and are believed to be correct, that the suit property is situate within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court, and that nothing material has been concealed therefrom. Verified at Bangalore on this the ____ day of __________ 2024.

                                                        {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

                                                        PLAINTIFF

Through:
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate
Enrollment No. {{ADVOCATE_ENROLLMENT}}
{{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}

    (a) Pass a decree for recovery of possession in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant directing the Defendant to deliver vacant and peaceful possession of the suit property, being immovable property bearing Survey No. 45, Koramangala, Bangalore, more particularly described in the schedule hereunder written;

    (b) Pass a preliminary decree for mesne profits in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for the period from 01.01.2023 till the date of delivery of possession at the rate of Rs. 25,000/- (Rupees Twenty-Five Thousand Only) per month, and direct an inquiry under Order XX Rule 12 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 for determination of the final amount of mesne profits;

    (c) Award costs of this suit including court fees, advocate fees and all pre-litigation costs incurred for issuing the legal notice;

    (d) Grant such other and further relief as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

AND FOR THIS ACT OF KINDNESS, THE PLAINTIFF SHALL, AS IN DUTY BOUND, EVER PRAY.

blocking_issues.issue: Cause of action anchored to a procedural step rather than the date when the right to sue actually accrued under the applicable law
blocking_issues.fix: Remove Paragraph 19's suggestion that cause of action arose on 15.03.2023 (legal notice date). The cause of action accrued on 01.01.2023 when the lease expired and Defendant's right to occupy ended. Procedural steps like legal notices are not accrual events for limitation purposes.

blocking_issues.issue: Indian Easements Act, 1882 Sections 60-63 cited for a lease/tenancy matter - this provision governs easementary rights (like right of way), not leasehold/tenancy interests
blocking_issues.fix: Remove Paragraph 17 entirely. The Indian Easements Act does not apply to lease/tenancy disputes. The correct legal basis is Transfer of Property Act (Sections 106, 111, 116) and Specific Relief Act (Section 5) which are already cited.

non_blocking_issues.issue: Excessive repetition of phrases throughout the draft affecting readability
non_blocking_issues.fix: Vary language for repeated phrases such as 'the suit property', 'of the suit', 'in unauthorized occupation', 'cause of action', 'the Plaintiff is', 'the Defendant is'. Use pronouns and alternative phrasing where appropriate.

final_artifacts.title: Suit for Recovery of Possession of Immovable Property with Mesne Profits
final_artifacts.text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT

AT BANGALORE

O.S. No. __________ / 2024

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}},
Aged about {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}},
Aged about {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF POSSESSION OF IMMOVABLE PROPERTY WITH MESNE PROFITS UNDER SECTION 5 OF THE SPECIFIC RELIEF ACT, 1963 READ WITH SECTION 26 OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the suit property, being immovable property bearing Survey No. 45, Koramangala, Bangalore, is situate within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court, and the Defendant is in unauthorized occupation of the said property within such territorial limits.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. 50,00,000/- (Rupees Fifty Lakhs Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court. This Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain suits for recovery of possession of immovable property.

FACTS OF THE CASE

4. The Plaintiff is the absolute owner of the immovable property bearing Survey No. 45, Koramangala, Bangalore, more particularly described in the schedule hereunder written (hereinafter referred to as "the suit property"). The Plaintiff has derived title to the suit property through valid ownership documents (copies of the ownership documents are annexed hereto and marked as Annexure A).

5. The Defendant is an erstwhile tenant whose tenancy stands determined. The Defendant was inducted as a tenant under a lease agreement in respect of the suit property for a period of three years commencing from 01.01.2020 and ending on 31.12.2022 (a copy of the lease/permission document granting permissive possession is annexed hereto and marked as Annexure B).

6. The said lease in favour of the Defendant stood determined and expired by efflux of time on 31.12.2022. Upon expiry of the lease period, the Defendant's right to occupy the suit property ceased, and the Defendant became liable to deliver vacant and peaceful possession of the suit property to the Plaintiff.

7. However, despite the expiry of the lease on 31.12.2022, the Defendant has wrongfully continued in occupation of the suit property without any lawful authority, right or title. The Defendant is in unauthorized occupation of the suit property from 01.01.2023 onwards.

8. The Plaintiff, through his advocate, caused a legal notice dated 15.03.2023 to be served upon the Defendant, calling upon the Defendant to vacate and deliver vacant possession of the suit property to the Plaintiff (a true copy of the legal notice along with the postal receipt and acknowledgment card is annexed hereto and marked as Annexure C).

9. Despite receipt of the said legal notice, the Defendant has failed, neglected and refused to vacate the suit property or to deliver possession thereof to the Plaintiff. The Defendant continues to be in wrongful possession of the suit property without any lawful justification.

10. The monthly rental value of the suit property is Rs. 25,000/-. The Plaintiff is entitled to mesne profits for the wrongful use and occupation of the suit property by the Defendant from 01.01.2023 onwards at the rate of Rs. 25,000/- per month.

11. The Plaintiff has clear title to the suit property and the Defendant's occupation is unauthorized. The Defendant has no right, title or interest in the suit property and is merely a trespasser whose tenancy has been lawfully determined under Section 111 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882.

LEGAL BASIS

12. The Plaintiff submits that he is entitled to recover possession of the suit property under Section 5 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963. Section 5 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963 provides that a person entitled to the possession of specific immovable property may recover the same in the manner prescribed by the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908. In the present case, the Plaintiff, being the owner of the suit property, is entitled to possession thereof, and the Defendant, being an erstwhile tenant whose tenancy has expired, is bound to deliver possession.

13. The Plaintiff further submits that the Defendant was a tenant whose tenancy has been lawfully determined under Section 111 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882. Section 111 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882 provides for the determination of lease, inter alia, by efflux of time. The lease in favour of the Defendant having expired on 31.12.2022, the tenancy stood determined by efflux of time, and the Defendant's continued possession is unauthorized.

14. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 106 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882 which provides for the duration of certain leases in the absence of a written contract or local custom. However, in the present case, the lease was for a fixed term which has expired by efflux of time.

15. The Plaintiff further relies upon Section 116 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882 which provides for the effect of holding over after the expiry of the lease. The Defendant, by continuing in possession after the determination of the lease, has become a tenant at will or a tenant holding on a month-to-month basis, and the Plaintiff is entitled to evict the Defendant.

16. The Plaintiff submits that he is entitled to claim mesne profits under Section 2(12) of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 read with Order XX Rule 12 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908. Section 2(12) of the CPC defines mesne profits as profits which the person in wrongful possession of such property actually received or might have received with ordinary diligence from it. The Defendant being in wrongful possession of the suit property, the Plaintiff is entitled to mesne profits at the rate of Rs. 25,000/- per month, being the fair rental value of the suit property.

CAUSE OF ACTION

17. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 01.01.2023 when the Defendant, despite the expiry of the lease on 31.12.2022, failed and refused to vacate the suit property and deliver vacant possession thereof to the Plaintiff, thereby continuing in unauthorized occupation and giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to sue for recovery of possession.

18. The cause of action is a continuing one as the Defendant continues to be in wrongful possession of the suit property from day to day, and the said cause of action continues to accrue and subsist from day to day within the territorial jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court.

LIMITATION

19. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 65 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of twelve years for a suit for possession of immovable property based on previous ownership or possession when the defendant is in unauthorized occupation after the expiry of a lease. The cause of action having first arisen on 01.01.2023 when the Defendant's tenancy determined, and the suit being filed within the prescribed period of twelve years from the said date, the present suit is well within limitation.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

20. The suit is valued at Rs. 50,00,000/- (Rupees Fifty Lakhs Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee, being the market value of the suit property. The requisite court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- has been paid on this plaint in accordance with the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958, computed on the suit valuation at the applicable ad valorem rate.

MESNE PROFITS

21. The Plaintiff claims mesne profits for the wrongful use and occupation of the suit property by the Defendant as follows:

    (a) Past mesne profits from 01.01.2023 till the date of filing of this suit at the rate of Rs. 25,000/- (Rupees Twenty-Five Thousand Only) per month, being the fair monthly rental value of the suit property;

    (b) Future mesne profits from the date of filing of this suit till the date of delivery of possession at the rate of Rs. 25,000/- (Rupees Twenty-Five Thousand Only) per month or at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems just and reasonable;

    (c) The Plaintiff reserves the right to claim enhanced mesne profits if the actual profits received or receivable by the Defendant are found to be higher than the claimed rate.

PRAYER

22. In the premises stated hereinabove, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be graciously pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree for recovery of possession in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant directing the Defendant to deliver vacant and peaceful possession of the suit property, being immovable property bearing Survey No. 45, Koramangala, Bangalore, more particularly described in the schedule hereunder written;

    (b) Pass a preliminary decree for mesne profits in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for the period from 01.01.2023 till the date of delivery of possession at the rate of Rs. 25,000/- (Rupees Twenty-Five Thousand Only) per month, and direct an inquiry under Order XX Rule 12 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 for determination of the final amount of mesne profits;

    (c) Award costs of this suit including court fees, advocate fees and all pre-litigation costs incurred for issuing the legal notice;

    (d) Grant such other and further relief as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

AND FOR THIS ACT OF KINDNESS, THE PLAINTIFF SHALL, AS IN DUTY BOUND, EVER PRAY.

SCHEDULE OF PROPERTY

All that piece and parcel of immovable property bearing Survey No. 45, situated at Koramangala, Bangalore, more particularly described as follows:

BOUNDARIES:
North : {{BOUNDARY_NORTH}}
South : {{BOUNDARY_SOUTH}}
East : {{BOUNDARY_EAST}}
West : {{BOUNDARY_WEST}}

Measuring: {{PROPERTY_AREA}} sq. ft. approximately.

|---------|-------------|----------|

| 2. | Lease/Permission Document granting permissive possession from 01.01.2020 to 31.12.2022 | Annexure B |
| 3. | Legal notice dated 15.03.2023 with postal receipt and AD card | Annexure C |

VERIFICATION

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby solemnly verify and state on oath that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 22 of this plaint are true and correct to my personal knowledge based upon the records and documents available with me, that the contents relating to legal submissions are based upon legal advice received and are believed to be correct, that the suit property is situate within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court, and that nothing material has been concealed therefrom. Plaintiff is owner of the property situated at Survey No. 45, Koramangala, Bangalore and Defendant is in unauthorized occupation after expiry of permissive possession/lease granted on 01.01.2020 which expired on 31.12.2022. Defendant refusing to vacate despite legal notice dated 15.03.2023. Property value is Rs.50,00,000/-. Monthly rental value is Rs.25,000/-. Seek recovery of possession, mesne profits from 01.01.2023 at Rs.25,000/- per month, and costs of the suit. File before the City Civil Court, Bangalore.
